title: Executive Assistant
description:
**Job Description**
*At Electrolux, as a leading global appliance company, we strive every day to shape living for the better for our consumers, our people and our planet. We share ideas and collaborate so that together, we can develop solutions that deliver enjoyable and sustainable living.*
*Come join us as you are. We believe diverse perspectives make us stronger and more innovative. In our global community of people from 100+ countries, we listen to each other, actively contribute and grow together.*
*Join us in our exciting quest to build the future home!*
**About the role:**
 Electrolux Group seeks an experienced
 **Executive Assistant** 
 to proactively provide high-level administrative support to two C-level executives.
   

  

 This is a critical role, and you will often be the first point of contact with people from inside and outside the organization. You will contribute to the efficiency of the overall business by ensuring all assigned administrative duties are carried out timely and efficiently. The work is highly collaborative and entails interacting with Senior Executives around the globe.
   

  

 This role requires high discretion and confidentiality as you will have detailed knowledge of the organization's operations, procedures, and people.
   

  

**Where you'll be:**
 This is a full-time position, based at Kungsholmen in Stockholm, Sweden.
   

  

 We work with 20% work-place flexibility, fostering collaboration and a strong team spirit at our Global Headquarters.
   

  

 We are 60+ nationalities under the same roof and we enjoy the look and feel of the office! You will be part of a dynamic international team where English is the natural language.
   

  

**Key responsibilities:**
* Schedule management: proactively coordinate and maintain the executive’s calendar, schedule meetings, and organize events
* Communication handling: review, prioritize, and respond to emails; act as liaison between the executive and internal/external stakeholders
* Travel arrangements: plan and book travel, accommodations, and handle travel-related expenses
* High-level administrative support: provide support such as approvals of SAP invoices, Concur expense reports, IT requests
* Meeting support: coordinate and deliver logistics for meetings, video conferences, gatherings, events etc.
* Interface with group management: communicate, collaborate and coordinate with group management executive assistants
* Interface with external stakeholders and their executive assistants (communication, coordination of meetings and travel etc.)


**About you:**
* Proactive & Solutions-Oriented: you anticipate issues before they arise and take initiative to address them
* Adaptable & Resilient: you adjust to changing circumstances and handle unexpected challenges with ease
* Stakeholder Management: Manages stakeholder relationships effectively
* Relationship: you show the ability to build strong, trust-based relationships and foster a collaborative team environment.
* Cross Collaboration & Networking. You collaborate with other executive assistants across our business and thereby make information flow easily while not compromising on discretion and confidentiality.
* Continuous learning: you seek opportunities for professional development to stay updated with technology and best practices


**Requirements:**
* Proven experience from a multinational business company as a Senior Executive Assistant with solid experience in supporting senior executive management
* Ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks simultaneously
* Excellent planning and organizing skills, attention to detail
* Experience in managing highly confidential information professionally
* Good knowledge of Microsoft Office 365
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills
* Fluent in English, both verbal and written. Intermediate Swedish is nice to have


 As an Electrolux Group employee, we will continuously invest in you and your development. There are no barriers to where your career could take you.

title: Senior Project Manager (Brands)
description:
**About Creed Media**
 Creed Media Group is a leading global creator marketing agency in music and entertainment. Started in Stockholm in 2018, today we work with the world’s biggest artists, labels, platforms, and brands to engineer the moments that break culture and convert attention into measurable growth.
   

  

 We run two business units. Music works with record labels and artist managers. Brands works with partners who hire us to turn culture into brand outcomes. Today, we are a trusted agency to some of the biggest names in music, entertainment, and consumer brands.
   

  

*Our mission: To Master Attention and Break Culture.*
**The Role**
 We’re bringing on a Senior Project Manager to own client engagements end to end, from strategy through delivery. You are the client’s primary day to day partner, the person they trust to translate ambition into a plan and a plan into results.
   

  

 You carry full ownership of scope, team, budget, and quality. You don’t wait for direction, you spot where a client relationship can grow and you shape the next opportunity yourself, in partnership with the Head of Brands. You move comfortably between the strategic conversation and the operational one, one moment framing a communication strategy, the next making sure a production deadline holds.
   

  

 Every client is different. Some want sharp, ongoing social content. Others want a full strategic launch plan for a brand or product. You read which one you’re in, come up with the ideas that get you there, and hold everyone, client, artist, creators, and your own team, together to make it happen.
   

  

 This is a role for someone who wants a portfolio of real client relationships, not a queue of tickets.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Own the strategic and operational relationship with assigned clients as their main point of contact.
* Lead client communication, align on objectives, manage expectations, and drive clear decision making.
* Spot and shape growth opportunities with existing clients, from proposal to pricing recommendation.
* Lead or facilitate strategic development of communication and marketing plans together with the Head of Brands.
* Own planning, execution, and evaluation of campaigns and always on initiatives, on time, on budget, at Creed quality.
* Own budgets, resourcing, and profitability tracking for your engagements.
* Safeguard creative quality across the process, from idea to final approval.
* Lead, coach, and develop team members and direct reports.
* Establish KPIs together with client and team, and turn performance data into recommendations.
* Produce recurring client reporting on outcomes, learnings, and next opportunities.


 Cadence: weekly pipeline and account review with Head of Brands, recurring client reporting cycles (e.g. quarterly).
   

  

 This role is for someone who treats a client relationship like a craft, who reads a brief and immediately sees ways to make the work better, and who gets real energy from leading a room, whether that room is a client, a creative team, or both at once.
   

  

**What Sits Outside The Role**
* New business hunting on unassigned accounts (separate BD function, separate quota).
* Final commercial or pricing strategy at company level.


**Who You Are**
**Must have:**
* Solid experience leading client engagements end to end, ideally within a creative, entertainment, or marketing agency.
* Track record owning budgets, timelines, and profitability on your projects.
* Comfortable leading both the strategic conversation and the operational delivery, not just one or the other.
* Experience leading and developing a team, direct reports or project teams.
* Strong stakeholder management, internal and client facing, without escalation.
* Interest across music, entertainment, and consumer brands. You don’t need deep expertise in all three, but you’re genuinely curious about how culture and commerce meet.
* Stockholm based and on site.
* Fluent Swedish and English.


**Nice To Have**
* A creative eye. You can come up with the idea for what to shoot or make, not just manage the brief. You understand what good looks like on social and in advertising, even when you’re not the one behind the camera or in the edit.
* Comfortable holding together everyone in the room, client, artist, creators, internal team, suppliers, making sure the right people are in the right place at the right time, while still driving the strategic thinking.


**What We Offer**
* A real portfolio to build. You won’t inherit a static book of accounts, you’ll grow the relationships you own and shape what they become.
* A platform to lead from. Creed’s track record with some of the biggest names in music, entertainment, and brands gives your work real weight from day one.
* Direct visibility. You’ll work closely with the Head of Brands, and the work you lead will be visible at company level.
* A team in motion. Things move fast, priorities shift, and you’ll often operate with incomplete information. If that energizes you, you’ll fit.
* You’ll own strategy, the creatives, and delivery together today. As Brands grows, so will the team around you.


**How To Apply**
 We welcome applications from both candidates seeking employment and registered sole traders and freelancers. Send us your resume and a short note on why you’re the right person to lead this.

title: IT Onsite Supporttekniker inom AV
description:
Om rollen
   

  

 Som IT Onsite Supporttekniker blir du en viktig del av verksamhetens lokala IT-support och fungerar som IT ansikte utåt. Du arbetar nära användarna och erbjuder snabb, professionell och pedagogisk hjälp i deras dagliga arbete.
   

  

 En central del av rollen är att säkerställa att konferensrum, mötesrum och AV-lösningar fungerar stabilt och effektivt. Du hanterar incidenter kopplade till bland annat ljud, bild, kameror, skärmar, dockningsstationer, presentationsteknik och digitala möteslösningar.
   

  

 Du kommer även att arbeta i verksamhetens Walk-in Servicedesk, där du hjälper användare med IT-relaterade frågor, incidenter, beställningar och enklare felsökning.
   

  

 Arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 I rollen kommer du bland annat att:
   

  

* Ge professionell och användarnära IT-support på plats.
* Vara första kontaktpunkt vid incidenter och frågor kopplade till konferensrum och AV-teknik.
* Felsöka problem med ljud, bild, kameror, mikrofoner, skärmar och mötesutrustning.
* Stötta användare vid Microsoft Teams-möten och andra digitala möteslösningar.
* Säkerställa att konferens- och mötesrum är funktionella och redo att användas.
* Arbeta i Walk-in Servicedesk och hantera incidenter, service requests och beställningar.
* Registrera, dokumentera och följa upp ärenden i ärendehanteringssystem.
* Skapa och uppdatera instruktioner, guider och dokumentation.
* Samarbeta med andra IT- och supportfunktioner vid större incidenter och driftstörningar.
* Bidra med förbättringsförslag som utvecklar supportflöden och användarupplevelsen.


 Vi söker dig som
   

  

 Har erfarenhet av användarnära IT-support och ett tydligt intresse för AV-teknik, konferensrumslösningar och service. Du trivs med att möta människor dagligen och kan kombinera teknisk felsökning med ett lugnt och professionellt bemötande.
   

  

 För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av onsite support, IT-support, servicedesk eller liknande användarnära roll.
* Kunskap om eller erfarenhet av AV-teknik och konferensrumslösningar.
* Erfarenhet av felsökning av skärmar, kameror, ljudutrustning och digitala mötesverktyg.
* Vana av att arbeta i ärendehanteringssystem och dokumentera genomförda åtgärder.
* God kommunikativ förmåga på svenska och engelska, i både tal och skrift.
* Förmåga att arbeta självständigt, strukturerat och lösningsorienterat.
* B-körkort.


 Meriterande
   

  

* Erfarenhet av konferensrumsteknik i en större organisation.
* Erfarenhet av Microsoft Teams Rooms eller liknande mötesrumslösningar.
* Grundläggande nätverkskunskaper kopplade till uppkopplad AV-utrustning.
* Erfarenhet av VIP-support eller stöd till ledningsnära funktioner.


 Som person är du
   

  

 Du är serviceinriktad, lyhörd och trygg i kontakten med användare. Du är praktiskt lagd och tycker om att lösa tekniska problem samtidigt som du skapar en positiv användarupplevelse.
   

  

 Du behåller lugnet även när tempot är högt, tar ansvar för hela ärendeflödet och har förmåga att prioritera mellan flera uppgifter. Du är prestigelös, samarbetar gärna med andra och delar med dig av din kunskap.
   

  

 Stor vikt kommer att läggas vid personlig lämplighet.
   

  

 Om uppdraget
   

  

* Omfattning: 100%
* Placering: Solna
* Period: 2 november 2026 – 1 november 2027
* Uppdraget utförs på plats


 Urval och intervjuer sker löpande. Välkommen med din ansökan redan idag!

title: Associate Media Specialist
description:
**📍Stockholm**
**Please Note:** 
 Due to the summer holiday period, our response times may be longer than usual. We appreciate your patience and look forward to reviewing your application.
   

  

 At Precis, we are a group of friendly experts in media buying, analytics & marketing science, creative and technology with a joint mission to help great companies thrive in the digital landscape.
   

  

 Are you curious to learn more about us? Read more about us here. For insights into what life is like at Precis and a peek at the perks of working with us, visit this page. In our Handbook, you can get a neat summary of who we are, our culture and our offices.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing 💼**
* Supporting your team with the planning, execution and reporting of campaigns across paid search, programmatic and paid social accounts.
* Conducting research and analyzing data to make data-driven decisions on campaign strategy and optimization.
* Assisting in the measurement and reporting of digital marketing KPIs, such as website traffic, lead generation, and conversion rates to guide our clients towards data-driven decisions.
* Staying up-to-date with the latest digital marketing trends, tools, and techniques, and making recommendations for new strategies or tactics.


**What We Are Looking For ✔️**
* Diploma in information systems, computer science, economics, statistics, digital marketing or similar.
* An enthusiasm to work with digital marketing based on passion, practical experience, or curiosity.
* Love of data – strong analytical and numerical skills, including a good rapport with Google Sheets or Excel.
* A friendly personality with good collaboration skills, plenty of ambition, and a desire to become a real expert in digital marketing.
* Experience managing paid search and/or paid social campaigns (preferred)
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.


**What We Offer🌟**
 You can read our full offering in our handbook.
   

  

* An intense learning environment where you will be able to grow and develop your skills and passions alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry.
* A culture that is built on collaboration, trust and innovation. We value having fun together - that’s why we also have regular afterworks, team events and a yearly festival.
* Transparent salary setting, you can find all our salary bands in our handbook. This role is an L1 role within the Media column. Check it out!
* 30 days paid leave, flexible public holidays, parental pay, a solid pension scheme and key insurance. Our different offices also offer different perks such as massage, health care contributions, free book orders, healthy snacks & great coffee, EAP programs and your choice of PC or Mac.
* A diverse environment with a high focus on inclusion and belonging. We speak over 27 native languages around Precis and our internal inclusion score is 4.5 out of 5.


 Please send your application in English.
   

  

 We are committed to building an inclusive and fair workplace that attracts talent and advances its people, regardless of their background, experiences or perspectives. We believe that a diverse set of minds will continue to contribute to our innovative culture and our ability to reinvent ourselves constantly.
   

  

 We update all candidates on their application by email. If you don't receive an email from us, please check your spam folder.

title: NDR Product Specialist
description:
**Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for an** 
**NDR Product Specialist** 
**to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Managed Services domain.**
**About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company**
 Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.
   

  

 Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.
   

  

 Today, we employ around 400 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for an NDR Product Specialist to our Product team, who wants to make a difference in our Managed Security Services domain.
   

  

**The Role**
 Join our Product Specialist team as an NDR Specialist, where you’ll take ownership of the technical landscape across our network detection and response capabilities. This hybrid role blends deep cyber security expertise with both internal and external enablement, ensuring our NDR platforms are optimally configured, continuously improved, and fully leveraged by our Security Operations teams and our customers. You will work primarily with Vectra and Darktrace to strengthen detection coverage, improve response workflows, and drive operational value from our NDR service offering.
   

  

 In this role, you will:
   

  

* Act as the technical platform specialist for NDR solutions, with a focus on Vectra and Darktrace, including deployment strategies, use case development, detection tuning, and operational best practices.
* Monitor, assess, and communicate product updates, new capabilities, and roadmap changes from NDR vendors to ensure our detection capabilities remain effective and relevant.
* Enable and educate – share your expertise by keeping SOC analysts and internal stakeholders up to date on NDR platform developments, detection opportunities, and best practices. Translate technical changes into actionable guidance and ensure knowledge is embedded in our processes.
* Work closely with vendors, engineering teams, and internal stakeholders to troubleshoot issues, optimize deployments, and increase detection and response capabilities, representing the Product team with clarity and credibility.
* Drive ongoing enhancements across our NDR tooling and processes, ensuring that new platform capabilities are rapidly and effectively integrated into our managed detection service.


**Your Potential Background**
 We are looking for someone with a passion for cybersecurity, a strong sense of product ownership, and a collaborative mindset. You thrive in a dynamic environment, are eager to stay current with the latest developments in network detection and response, and enjoy sharing your expertise with others. You have 2-5 years of hands-on experience in cybersecurity, ideally as a product specialist within NDR, a technical account manager, a detection engineer, or in a similar role within a SOC or MSSP environment.
   

  

**Required Skills:**
* Deep familiarity with NDR platforms, including deployment strategies, detection engineering, and advanced technical capabilities within tools such as Vectra and Darktrace.
* Experience with operationalizing NDR solutions in enterprise, SOC, or MSSP environments.
* Strong interest in technical product ownership, with a proactive approach to learning and staying current with evolving NDR features, detection methods, and best practices.
* Ability to communicate complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
* Collaborative and outgoing personality; comfortable representing the team in cross-functional meetings, vendor dialogues, and technical customer discussions.
* Self-motivated, with a proven ability to work independently.


**Bonus points if you have:**
* Knowledge of vendor licensing models, service packaging, or commercial considerations related to NDR platforms.
* Experience writing detection rules, configuring automated workflows, integrating APIs, or tuning detections within Vectra, Darktrace, or similar NDR platforms.
* Relevant cybersecurity certifications or equivalent practical experience in network security, threat detection, or security operations.
* Previous experience working in a SOC or MSSP environment.


*Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.*
**Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future**
 If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.
   

  

 So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

title: Associate Business Developer Manager
description:
*Want to influence the bigger picture? At Saab, you can realize amazing innovations that keep people and societies safe. Together, we can truly make an impact – and your part matters.*
 Your role
   

  

 We are looking for a Associate Business Development Manager to join our Marketing & Sales team responsible for sales of Saab Airborne Early Warning & Control system GlobalEye. This position offers a unique opportunity to be part of our organization's journey and transformation as we grow our global footprint and develop our ways of working.
   

  

 You will work on complex sales in the highly competitive international defense market, providing the opportunity to work with our world-leading portfolio and key areas for the company’s future. This means being able to manage complex, large-scale offerings across our product portfolio for new and existing customers.
   

  

 You will join a team working in close cooperation with all relevant business units within Saab to sell and secure complex contracts on a large scale. You will have close interaction with the different programs and product owners, business development and strategy team, finance and commercial as well as Saab's top management to define and execute a successful win-strategy to develop and secure business.
   

  

 Travelling is required, mainly outside Sweden. These trips can be related both to specific customer interactions as well as general marketing events such as exhibitions and trade shows.
   

  

 In our group, all members contribute with their individual experiences and skills to create an effective and dynamic environment. You are expected not only to work with your individual area of responsibility, but being a member of the team also means that you interact and take part in other activities and tasks within the unit.
   

  

 Your profile
   

  

 We are looking for a Associate Business Development Manager with a curious mindset about how to do business and a keen interest in developing their sales and leadership skills in an international context. The candidate should have some experience participating in complex projects, such as acquisitions, expansions, or the establishment of international sites. Experience in technically intensive sectors, such as engineering or project-driven operations, is preferred. Additionally, the candidate should demonstrate strong numerical data handling and presentation skills, coupled with a solid understanding of business models.
   

  

 As a person, you have an outstanding ability to develop relationships and networks, but also to keep focus on pushing sales efforts forward until closing a contract. You also have a passion for innovation in both technology and business models. Analytical skills are very important not only to understand the customer requirements and system solutions, but also to manage all the financial aspects of our proposals. Excellent presenting and communication skills are mandatory, as well as leadership skills to engage and lead bid teams successfully.
   

  

 Since you will mainly engage with international customers, strong English skills in a business environment, both orally and in writing, are mandatory. It is also preferable to have Swedish skills. The successful candidate will be based at the Saab Solna Strand site.
   

  

 We also assume you have:
   

  

* A technical or business degree from a university
* 2-5 years of work experience in business development and/or management consulting in an industry and international environment
* The ability to combine strategic thinking with operational actions
* Interest in moving ideas to revenue, analyzing business models, markets, partners, and competition
* Interest in leading, developing, and challenging the organization, management teams, and employees
* Cooperative skills, drive, and the ability to generate energy and motivate team members
* Persistence and dedication to handle long-term marketing and sales initiatives


 Desired experience includes:
   

  

* Management consulting
* Complex industry set-ups


 This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.
   

  

 What you will be part of
   

  

 Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.
   

  

 Saab is a leading defense and security company with an enduring purpose, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 28,000 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.
   

  

 Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defense capability of several nations. Read more about us here
   

  

*Kindly observe that this is an ongoing recruitment process and that the position might be filled before the closing date of the advertisement.*

title: Service Delivery Manager
description:
Location: Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

 Thales people architect identity management and data protection solutions at the heart of digital security. Business and governments rely on us to bring trust to the billions of digital interactions they have with people. Our technologies and services help banks exchange funds, people cross borders, energy become smarter and much more. More than 30,000 organizations already rely on us to verify the identities of people and things, grant access to digital services, analyze vast quantities of information and encrypt data to make the connected world more secure.
   

  

 "As market leader within Digital Identity and Security, Thales makes personal digital interaction simple and secure in an increasingly connected society. From secure software to biometrics and encryption, Thales provides technologies and services that enables business and governments to authenticate identities and protect data, enabling its clients to offer trusted digital services to billions of individuals. Located in Älvsjö since 2012, the site houses both a commercial office as well as two productions within Identity & Biometric Solutions, and Banking & Payment Services. Working commercially across all business areas within Digital Identity and Security, Thales has become a trusted partner within various businesses as well as governments throughout the Nordics.
   

  

**Role: Service Delivery Manager**
**Base location: Stockholm, Sweden (Hybrid)**
**Security process:** 
 This position requires that you undergo and be approved in accordance with the applicable regulations for security protection. For positions where Thales has requirements for security clearance, this may entail a requirement for local citizenship.
   

  

**Identity & Biometric Solutions (IBS) business line**
 Thales Identity and Biometric Solutions (IBS) Business Line is part of the global Thales Group, which employs more than 80,000 people in 68 countries.
   

  

 Thales delivers identity and biometric solutions to governments, public authorities, and private entities in civil identity and public security. We do this by providing highly secure documents, such as passports, ID cards, driver’s licenses, etc., at the heart of identity schemes. We also provide solutions for verifying people’s identities, for example, when they cross borders and enrolling them in national registers. Both of these are built on our expertise in biometrics.
   

  

 And, as governments go through a digital transformation, we support them by delivering secure, convenient, and feature-proof mobile identity, digital identity wallet, platform, and services. We also address public security and law enforcement challenges by offering the latest in criminal forensics and real-time facial recognition.
   

  

**Description**
 Thales Cybersecurity & Digital & Identity (CDI) provides Customers with technology to assist them in Identity and Biometric Solutions (IBS) business line.
   

  

 Service Delivery Manager (SDM) shall manage the support and maintenance contracts related to Government Solutions (including Software and equipment supplied by Thales as well as third parties). SDM will ensure customer satisfaction in respect of contractual commitments, and the fulfilment of Service level agreements (SLA). SDM will be mainly involved in governance of incident, problem, and change management on the complete solution with contribution to upsell and new opportunities.
   

  

 The installed Biometric and Public Security Solutions with associated software, hardware and peripherals are integrated with the Customers processes. SDM work with the Customer, Thales Internal and External Partner Suppliers and Thales Development/Senior Technical Teams (Level 2 and Level 3 Support) to ensure our response and restoration processes are aligned to contractual SLA and Availability and Reliability are maximised.
   

  

 The role will work closely with different departments at our Älvsjö Office. The Line Manager is located abroad and so is several of the other Service Delivery Managers in the Manager’s team.
   

  

**Key responsibilities include:**
* Act as the primary customer interface for Support and Maintenance (S&M) contracts.
* Manage customer accounts by providing regular activity reporting, including weekly and monthly updates, steering committee materials, KPI reports, and SLA reports; recommend service improvements; and support contract renewals and new commercial proposals in coordination with Sales.
* Monitor incident resolution, ensure SLA compliance, and manage escalations whenever required.
* Monitor contract financial performance and maintain a strong focus on customer satisfaction.
* Manage third-party contracts, communications, and performance monitoring where applicable.
* Coordinate and follow up on actions involving third parties and internal support teams, ensuring alignment with Thales processes.
* Prepare, plan, and manage the execution of changes in coordination with technical teams.
* Coordinate contractual activities such as preventive maintenance operations.
* Provide internal reporting on account activities and profitability; manage the contract using Thales tools in accordance with Thales processes.


**Knowledge, Skills and Experience:**
* An engineering degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related field.
* At least five years of experience in service delivery management or project management for IT solutions.
* Strong customer relationship management skills.
* ITIL Foundation certification.
* Excellent communication skills in English and Swedish.
* Ability to travel at short notice within Europe.
* Eligibility for security clearance for Swedish customer.
* Experience working with remote teams.
* Experience in government-related activities is a plus.


**What we offer:**
* Competitive compensation package including base salary and performance-based incentives.
* A key role in shaping the company’s presence in a strategically important region.
* Opportunity to work with innovative technologies and cross-border teams.
* Professional development, training, and career advancement opportunities.


 At Thales we provide CAREERS and not only jobs. With Thales employing 80,000 employees in 68 countries our mobility policy enables thousands of employees each year to develop their careers at home and abroad, in their existing areas of expertise or by branching out into new fields. Together we believe that embracing flexibility is a smarter way of working. Great journeys start here, apply now!

title: Sales Development Representative
description:
Grow with us
   

  

 Upsales was founded in 2003, grew without a single outside investor, and has been profitable ever since. No VC pressure. No compromises. Just a product customers keep paying for.
   

  

 We build the leading CRM and AI platform for B2B sales, and we're now in our highest growth phase in years, one of the very few SaaS companies growing profitably while staying genuinely ahead on AI.
   

  

 If you want real autonomy, colleagues who push you, and work that moves the needle from day one, you'll fit right in.
   

  

 AI first mindset
   

  

 We’re not looking for someone who simply uses AI, we want someone who weaponizes it. You treat tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI copilots as force multipliers, allowing you to deliver at a level and speed others can’t match. For you, AI isn’t an add-on, it’s the default operating system for how you think, work, and lead. You’re relentlessly curious about new capabilities, and you push the limits of what’s possible, turning AI into a competitive advantage for yourself, Upsales, and our customers. This is a role for someone with real ambition: someone who wants to set a new standard for efficiency, precision, and impact in the age of AI.
   

  

 Who We're Looking For
   

  

 You’re experienced enough to know what great prospecting looks like, and curious enough to keep refining it. You’re competitive, thoughtful, and you don’t wait for things to happen, you make them happen.
   

  

**You Have**
* Minimum 2-3 years of experience in B2B sales, outbound prospecting, or business development (ideally SaaS or tech)
* A strong ability to research, prioritize, and engage senior stakeholders
* Proven success hitting or exceeding targets in a structured sales environment
* Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, in Swedish and English
* A results-driven mindset, combined with intellectual curiosity and a growth mentality
* A background in CRM, SaaS, or solution-oriented sales is a plus. More important is your ability to think strategically, execute with precision, and contribute to a winning team.


 This isn’t an entry-level role. And it’s not just about booking meetings. This is about strategic outreach, combining research, insight, and commercial creativity to identify, engage, and open doors with the right prospects. You’ll be building relationships with senior decision-makers, crafting compelling outreach, and shaping the very first impression of Upsales.
   

  

 You’ll be surrounded by high-performing sales and marketing colleagues, but you own your results. Success here isn’t just about hitting activity metrics, it’s about quality, precision, and the ability to create real pipeline opportunities that convert.
   

  

 This is a role for someone who thrives in a fast-paced, high-performance environment, who takes pride in mastering their craft, and who wants to grow into strategic software sales.
   

  

 Upsales was founded in 2003, grew without a single outside investor, and has been profitable ever since. No VC pressure. No compromises. Just a product customers keep paying for.
   

  

 We're now in our highest growth phase in years, one of the very few SaaS companies growing profitably while staying genuinely ahead on AI.
   

  

 If you want real autonomy, colleagues who push you, and work that moves the needle from day one, you'll fit right in.

title: Senior Operational Lead / Service Delivery Manager
description:
About Norvion Systems AB
  
Headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden, Norvion Systems AB is an innovative engineering company dedicated to deploying cutting-edge AI, robotics, and embedded systems solutions across the Nordic region and Europe. We bridge advanced technological innovation with successful real-world deployments, serving as Europe’s trusted engineering partner.
  
Our leadership and core technical teams comprise industry veterans and technical experts from global tech pioneers, Tier 1 suppliers, and intelligent cockpit fields. We cultivate a practical, reliable, and quality-driven Nordic engineering culture. At Norvion, you will work at the forefront of Embodied AI and advanced edge platform engineering, collaborating with top-tier research institutions and industrial leaders to push the limits of technology.
  
  

**The Opportunity:**  

To ensure the seamless delivery and robust operational performance of our core enterprise systems, we are currently supporting a premier client in Gothenburg in the search for an experienced Senior Operational Lead / Service Delivery Manager.
  
Key Focus Areas
  
In this role, you will bridge the gap between technical operations and business expectations, driving excellence across the following areas:
  
**Operational Excellence:** Leading operational delivery, driving continuous improvements, and ensuring high-availability service delivery in a modern DevOps environment.
  
**Incident & Coordination:** Orchestrating incident management, production support, and fast-paced operational coordination.
  
**Stakeholder & Customer Success:** Managing complex stakeholder environments and maintaining transparent, high-level customer communication.
  
**Team Leadership:** Coaching, mentoring, and guiding cross-functional teams toward operational maturity and collaborative success.
  
  

Profile & Qualifications
  
We are looking for a senior professional who thrives in dynamic environments and possesses a strong delivery mindset:
  
**Proven Leadership:** Extensive experience as a Service Delivery Manager, Operational Lead, or a closely related senior operational role.
  
**Tech Ecosystem Knowledge:** Hands-on understanding of DevOps environments, production support setups, and IT service management (ITSM) frameworks.
  
**Communication & Influence:** Exceptional stakeholder management skills and the ability to articulate complex technical status into clear customer communications.
  
**Language Skills:** Fluent in English (both written and spoken) is a mandatory requirement.
  
**Work Authorization:** The legal right to work in Sweden by the project start date (or possessing an active permit that allows an immediate start without a new application process) is strongly preferred due to the August 2026 timeline.
  
  

Why Choose Norvion?
  
**A Stage to Push Boundaries:** "Engineering Beyond Boundaries" — We provide direct, hands-on exposure to the future of advanced automotive systems, general-purpose robotics, and embodied intelligence.
  
**Work with Tech Experts:** Collaborate closely with a highly skilled engineering team boasting deep European delivery experience. No red tape, just pure engineering excellence.
  
**Nordic Work Culture:** Embrace a flexible, flat, and results-oriented environment that values work-life balance and individual growth.

title: Azure DevOps Specialist i Stockholm!
description:
Som Azure DevOps Specialist blir du en del av ett kompetent team med fokus på att utveckla och förvalta organisationens Azure DevOps-plattform. Här arbetar du med att skapa effektiva, säkra och skalbara leveransflöden som används av flera utvecklingsteam.
  
Du får en central roll i att driva automation, standardisering och kontinuerliga förbättringar genom hela DevSecOps-kedjan från kod och test till release och produktion.
  
Detta är ett hyrköpsuppdrag, vilket innebär att ambitionen är en långsiktig anställning hos kunden efter uppdragets slut.
  
️Dina arbetsuppgifter
  
  

**Du kommer bland annat att:**  

Bygga, utveckla och förvalta Azure DevOps-plattformen.
  
  

Designa och underhålla skalbara CI/CT/CD-pipelines.
  
  

Arbeta med Managed DevOps Pools och YAML-baserade pipelines.
  
  

Hantera Artifacts, feeds och pakethantering.
  
  

Säkerställa governance, tekniska ramverk och best practices.
  
  

Integrera säkerhet och testautomatisering i leveransflöden.
  
  

Stötta utvecklingsteam i användningen av Azure DevOps.
  
  

Driva kontinuerliga förbättringar inom automation och DevSecOps.
  
  

✅Vi söker dig som har
  
Minst 4 års erfarenhet som Azure DevOps Specialist.
  
  

Mycket god erfarenhet av Azure DevOps som plattform.
  
  

Erfarenhet av att bygga automatiserade leveransflöden från kod till test och drift.
  
  

God förståelse för DevSecOps och säker utveckling.
  
  

Erfarenhet av att skapa skalbara lösningar för flera utvecklingsteam.
  
  

Praktisk erfarenhet av YAML, Git, PowerShell och gärna C#.
  
  

⭐Meriterande
  
  

**Det är extra positivt om du har erfarenhet av:**  

.NET-utveckling (C#)
  
  

SAFe eller andra agila arbetssätt
  
  

Testautomatisering och CI/CD-ramverk
  
  

Säkerhetsskanning i pipelines, exempelvis SonarQube
  
  

Containerplattformar
  
  

Release on Demand och moderna utvecklingsplattformar
  
  

Vem är du?
  
  

**Vi tror att du är en person som:**  

Trivs med att samarbeta över teamgränser.
  
  

Har ett lösningsorienterat och proaktivt arbetssätt.
  
  

Delar gärna med dig av din kunskap.
  
  

Är nyfiken på ny teknik och moderna arbetssätt.
  
  

Känner dig bekväm i en rådgivande specialistroll.
  
  

Har ett stort fokus på kvalitet, säkerhet och automation.
  
  

Därför kommer du att trivas
  
Du blir en del av en organisation som ligger långt fram inom digital utveckling och investerar i moderna utvecklingsplattformar, automation och säkerhet. Här får du arbeta tillsammans med engagerade kollegor i en innovativ miljö där teknik, samarbete och ständig förbättring står i centrum.
  
Dessutom finns en tydlig ambition om en långsiktig anställning efter konsultperioden.
  
  

**Praktiska detaljer:**  

**⏳ Uppdragsperiod:** 2026-09-01 – 2027-02-28
  
**Omfattning:** 100% heltid
  
**Placering:** Stockholm (hybrid)
  
**Anställningsform:** Hyrköp (med ambition om överrekrytering)
  
Ansökan
  
Urval påbörjas efter sommarsemestrarna under vecka 33. Skicka därför gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt! Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!
  
Sway Sourcing är en innovativ rekryteringspartner som specialiserar sig på att matcha rätt talang med rätt företag – snabbt och effektivt. Vårt huvudfokus ligger inom Ekonomi, Administration, HR, Marknad och IT, men vi har även den breda expertis och flexibilitet som krävs för att leverera skräddarsydda rekryteringslösningar inom alla branscher.
  
Trots att vi är en relativt ny aktör har vi redan byggt förtroende hos många av Sveriges största företag och arbetar både nationellt och internationellt. Med baser i Sverige och Spanien erbjuder vi en unik kombination av lokal expertis och global räckvidd. Vårt starka nätverk och djupa branschinsikter gör oss till en självklar partner för företag som vill ligga steget före i sin rekrytering.

title: DevOps engineer – Loggplattform
description:
Vill du vara med och bidra till att skapa Sveriges bästa hälso-och sjukvård och ett hållbart samhälle med tillväxt i hela Västra Götaland? Västra Götalandsregionen är Sveriges största offentliga arbetsgivare med ambitionen att bli Sveriges bästa offentliga arbetsgivare. Hos oss får du chansen att utföra ett meningsfullt arbete för samhället och bidra till Västra Götalandsregionens vision ”det goda livet”. På koncernkontoret arbetar vi dagligen med att göra skillnad för patienter, medarbetare, besökare och invånare.


Nu söker vi en DevOps engineer med inriktning logghantering (befattning: utvecklingsingenjör) som vill vara med och utveckla och förvalta en gemensam loggplattform för ökad säkerhet, spårbarhet och insikt i Västra Götalandsregionens IT-miljö. Ta chansen och sök dig till en av Sveriges största arbetsgivare.**Ditt uppdrag**  

Som DevOps engineer med inriktning logghantering blir du en del av Koncernstab Digitalisering, område Plattformar och infrastruktur med fokus på säkerhetsplattformar.


Du kommer att arbeta i en öppen och agil organisation där samarbete, kunskapsdelning och kontinuerlig utveckling är en naturlig del av arbetet. Du ingår i ett produktteam bestående av produktägare, teamcoach och tekniska specialister. Vi söker dig som är nyfiken och har en stark vilja att utvecklas, samtidigt som du bidrar till teamet och stöttar dina kollegor. Rollen innebär många kontaktytor, vilket gör dialog och samarbete till en viktig del av din vardag.
Västra Götalandsregionen håller på att etablera en gemensam loggplattform för att möjliggöra insamling och analys av loggdata från olika källor. I din roll ansvarar du för att utveckla, förvalta och vidareutveckla plattformen, med fokus på automatisering, skalbarhet och säkerhet.


Rollen innebär att arbeta med insamling och övervakning av loggdata för att säkerställa hög säkerhet och full spårbarhet i våra system.


Dina arbetsuppgifter innefattar bland annat att:

* implementera och vidareutveckla loggplattformen i en Open Source-baserad miljö
* integrera loggflöden från olika system och plattformar
* utveckla, automatisera och effektivisera logghanteringen
* arbeta med och vidareutveckla befintlig SIEM-lösning
**Vad kan vi erbjuda dig?**

  

* Inspirerande arbetsmiljö med fokus på innovation och samarbete.
* Möjlighet att arbeta med spännande teknologier och bidra till betydelsefulla förändringsinitiativ.
* Stöd för din professionella utveckling och möjligheter till vidareutbildning.
* Engagerade och stödjande kollegor som värdesätter dina idéer och insatser.


Vi kan erbjuda dig ett utmanande och varierande jobb med stora individuella utvecklingsmöjligheter. Vi satsar mycket på kompetensutveckling, externa utbildningar och att ständigt hålla oss i teknikens framkant. Hos oss får du möjlighet att göra skillnad och utvecklas tillsammans med duktiga och erfarna kollegor.


Västra Götalandsregionen erbjuder flexibla arbetsformer utifrån verksamhetens förutsättningar och det finns möjlighet att kombinera arbete från våra kontor i Göteborg, Mölndal, Borås, Skövde eller Vänersborg samt på distans.

**För att lyckas i rollen behöver du:**

  

* Kandidatexamen i datavetenskap, informationsteknik, systemvetenskap eller annan relevant teknisk utbildning, alternativt motsvarande kompetens
* Flerårig erfarenhet inom DevOps, systemadministration eller mjukvaruutveckling
* Erfarenhet av logghantering i större miljöer samt av att arbeta med eller etablera Open Source-baserade plattformar
* Erfarenhet av centrala loggplattformar, exempelvis Elasticsearch/OpenSearch, Loki, VictoriaLogs eller liknande
* Goda kunskaper i Linux samt erfarenhet av systemadministration i både Linux- och Windowsmiljöer
* Erfarenhet av versionshantering, övervakning, loggning och skriptning
* God förståelse för nätverk, DNS, lastbalansering och andra centrala infrastrukturtjänster
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med säkerhet som en integrerad del av tekniska lösningar
* Kunskap inom informationssäkerhet och SIEM


Vi ser också att du:

* har ett stort teknikintresse och håller dig uppdaterad inom ny teknik
* är analytisk, strukturerad och lösningsorienterad
* trivs i en transparent och samarbetsinriktad miljö
* har god samarbetsförmåga och bidrar till teamets utveckling


Meriterande om du även kan:

* Certifierad Linux System Administrator
* Grafana och Prometheus
* Open Source databaser
* SQL
* OpenTelemetry
* Oauth2 och OIDC integration
* Envoy
* Ansible
* Utveckling med Python
* Go
* Git
* Pipeline verktyg till exempel logstash, fluentbit, fluentd,


Som person är du nyfiken, med en stark vilja att ständigt lära nytt, och gillar utmaningar, förändringar samt ser digitaliseringen och ny teknik som en självklarhet. Du är noggrann och van vid att ta eget ansvar, är drivande, lösningsorienterad och du har god samarbetsförmåga.


Rollen innebär många kontakter, så dialog och samarbete är viktiga delar i arbetet. Du har mycket god förmåga att kommunicera, såväl muntligt som skriftligt, på svenska och god förmåga när det gäller engelska. Tjänsten kan komma att bli säkerhetsklassad vilket kan ställa krav på svenskt medborgarskap.


Det är viktigt för oss att du kan arbeta utifrån vår värdegrund:

* Tillsammans gör vi varandra framgångsrika.
* Vi lyssnar in. Vi lyssnar av.
* Feedback bidrar till vår utveckling.

**Övrigt**  

Som en av Sveriges största arbetsgivare kan vi erbjuda rätt person goda karriär- och utvecklingsmöjligheter.


Vi eftersträvar jämlikhet för alla medarbetare, att de trivs på arbetsplatsen och värnar om en god balans mellan arbetsliv och privatliv.


Läs mer om vår verksamhet här: http://www.vgregion.se/om-vgr/


Om Västra GötalandsregionenHos oss blir du en del av en större helhet. Vi är 56 000 medarbetare i över 200 yrken som varje dag gör skillnad i människors liv i hela Västra Götaland. Du bidrar till en trygg och jämlik vård samt till ett hållbart samhälle där miljö, kultur och goda kommunikationer skapar livskvalitet. Vi är mitt i en digital utveckling där du får vara med och forma framtidens arbetssätt för att förbättra och förenkla vardagen för invånare och verksamheter. Som tillsvidareanställd blir du krigsplacerad som en del av vårt gemensamma ansvar för att samhällsviktiga funktioner ska fungera även i kris. Vill du veta mer om oss? På vår webb kan du läsa mer om våra förmåner och möta medarbetare som varje dag gör skillnad och utvecklas i sina yrken. https://www.vgregion.se/jobba-i-vgr/sa-ar-det-att-jobba-hos-oss/ AnsökanVästra Götalandsregionen ser helst att du registrerar din ansökan via rekryteringssystemet. Om du som sökande har frågor om den utannonserade tjänsten eller av särskilda och speciella skäl inte kan registrera dina uppgifter i ett offentligt system - kontakta kontaktperson för respektive annons. Till bemannings-, förmedlings- och rekryteringsföretag och till dig som är försäljare:Vi undanber oss vänligen men bestämt direktkontakt med bemannings-, förmedlings- och rekryteringsföretag samt andra externa aktörer och försäljare av ytterligare jobbannonser. Västra Götalandsregionen har upphandlade avtal.


Anställningsform: tillsvidareanställning | Anställningens omfattning: heltid | Antal lediga befattningar: 1 | Sysselsättningsgrad: 100% | Ort: Mölndal, Göteborg, Skövde, Borås, Vänersborg | Län: Västra Götalands län | Land: Sweden | Referensnummer: 2026/3717 | Kontakt: Jan Åke Hovenäs 0722499854, Micael Karlsson 070-3402068, | Facklig företrädare: Vision: Jeanette Hansson 076-7742849, Akademikerförbundet SSR, SACO: Git Olofsson 0722-063558, Akavia, SACO: Enis Kurspahic 079-0612078, Sv Ingenjörer: Yvonne Jagersjö 073-8048765, | Publicerat: 2026-07-13 | Sista ansökningsdag: 2026-08-10

title: Platform Engineer
description:
**About Froda** 




 Founded in 2015, Froda’s mission is to democratize access to financing, giving small businesses the same growth opportunities as large ones. Through digital innovation, we’ve transformed the loan process from months to minutes, offering fair, fast, and accessible financing. Now, through embedded lending, we’re integrating our technology with global banks and fintechs, building the next generation of lending infrastructure. Join us in creating opportunities for small businesses to invest in their ideas to the best possible terms.
 



  





**About the Role** 




 As Froda continues to scale, we’re looking for a Platform Engineer to join our Platform team — the team at the heart of our technology. The team’s mission is to build and maintain the internal platform that enables and supports our developers to deliver reliable, scalable, and secure fintech solutions efficiently.
 



  





 In this role, you’ll work closely with engineers across Froda to understand their needs and ensure they have the tools, environments, and automation required to succeed. You’ll be part of a small, high-impact team that values helping others, simplifying complexity, and improving developer experience through collaboration and support.
 



 Your work will directly impact how quickly and safely our product teams can innovate, making you a key enabler of Froda’s growth.
 



  





**Who You Are** 




 You’re a technically skilled engineer who takes pride in building robust, scalable systems that others can depend on. You see infrastructure as a shared foundation, one that requires both strong technical execution and close collaboration. You thrive in a team environment, value clear communication, and enjoy enabling others to move faster and more confidently.
 



  





 We’re looking for someone who:
 


* Thrives in a collaborative environment where support and communication are key.
* Enjoys solving infrastructure challenges and enabling others to succeed.
* Is proactive, approachable, and has a strong sense of ownership.
* Balances technical depth with a genuine interest in teamwork and shared success.



  





**Key Qualifications** 




**Experience:** 




 Experience in a Platform Engineering, DevOps, or Infrastructure role.
 



**Skills & Tools:** 



* **Infrastructure as Code:** 
 Experience with Terraform or similar IaC tools.
* **Cloud Platforms:** 
 Hands-on experience with Azure, GCP or AWS.
* **CI/CD:** 
 Ability to design, implement, and improve pipelines that serve multiple teams.
* **Containers & Orchestration:** 
 Strong understanding of Docker and Kubernetes in production.
* **Monitoring & Observability:** 
 Experience with tools that support proactive issue detection and team visibility.
* **Security:** 
 Awareness of cloud security best practices and shared responsibility models.



  





**What’s in it for You** 




 At Froda, you’ll be part of a company that combines the best of both worlds, a fast-moving, high-energy culture with the stability and structure to support long-term success. We’re 100+ ambitious self-starters who genuinely care for each other and the work we do, all while having fun together.
 



  





 Here, you’ll have the opportunity to take ownership of your work, develop your skills, and shape your own growth journey. With big ambitions and a rapidly evolving business, there’s plenty of room to grow, lead initiatives, and make a real impact on both our company and the industry. If you're looking to be part of a success story and contribute to something bigger, this is the place for you.
 



  





 Furthermore, you’ll get:
 


* An opportunity to be part of an exciting journey, shaping the future of our company and transforming the SME financing landscape
* Fabulous office overlooking Stockholm and a friendly culture that values work/life balance
* We prioritize working in the office to maximize the positive impact of in-person collaboration, but we offer flexibility with work hours and remote work
* Competitive compensation package
* Insurance and pension plan



  





 This role is all about building the tech that powers funding for thousands of entrepreneurs, if you're excited by our mission, apply or tell a friend!

title: Systems / Platform Engineer
description:
**About the Role:** 




  





 We are looking for a highly technical Systems/Platform Engineer to strengthen our Private Cloud team. In this role, you will apply software engineering principles to IT operations, building automated tooling and resilient infrastructure to ensure our large-scale systems stay highly available, fast and scalable.
 



  





 You will take ownership of the Kubernetes clusters and VMs running in our on-prem cloud, designing, implementing, and maintaining robust solutions across the estate. Your goal is to eliminate manual operational "toil," maximize automation, and ensure the high availability and performance of our applications.
 



  





 We view our infrastructure as a product and the development teams as our customers. We are looking for a systems engineer who builds with empathy, enjoys being a helpful technical partner, and focuses on enabling others rather than acting as a gatekeeper. As part of a small, senior, and deeply technical team, you will occupy a centra role—collaborating closely with Development, Hosting- and Network Infrastructure, GCP, and AWS teams in a dynamic, agile environment. We value curiosity, patience, and a constructive attitude. We believe that no question is too basic to deserve a helpful answer.
 



  





 We believe that "if DevOps is the philosophy, SRE/Systems Engineering is the implementation."
 



 Our stack is modern, complex, and heavily rooted in Open Source software:
 


* Infrastructure: Private Cloud, Virtualization, Containerization
* OS & Orchestration: Linux, Flatcar, Kubernetes (CAPI)
* Data & Messaging: SQL, NoSQL, Columnar Databases, Distributed Keystores, Messaging Services
* CI/CD & Automation: Ansible, Flux, Helm, Terraform, Github Action



  





**Responsibilities and impact:** 



* Infrastructure Design & Architecture: Architect and deploy resilient on-premise and cloud-based applications. Contribute to high-level architectural decisions to ensure infrastructure supports long-term growth and technical reliability.
* Extending Kubernetes via custom operators/controllers or API integrations.
* Automation & Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Embrace the "everything as code" philosophy. Write code to automate provisioning, deployment, and routine tasks using tools like Ansible and Terraform to manage fleets of servers efficiently.
* Incident Management & Security: Take a proactive role in vulnerability management (patching and hardening). Investigate production issues, triage major incidents, and participate in blameless postmortems to ensure continuous system evolution.
* Observability & Monitoring: Design and manage comprehensive monitoring and alerting solutions. Identify patterns in system behavior to preemptively address errors, track system health, and ensure maximum uptime.
* Capacity Planning & Support: Forecast infrastructure needs, perform load/disaster recovery testing, and partner with cross-functional engineering teams to translate complex infrastructure needs into actionable plans.



  





**What we’re looking for:** 




**Fundamental requirement:** 



* Background: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Systems Engineering, or equivalent experience, with 5–10 years of relevant experience in production environments.
* Operating Systems: Solid, deep understanding of Linux operating system internals (networking, storage, systemd, namespaces) and robust Linux scripting capabilities.
* Containers & Orchestration: Solid understanding of Cloud-native operations using Kubernetes (including Cluster API/CAPI, Helm, Flux, and Flatcar).
* Programming: Proficiency in building and maintaining tools in Go (or strong proficiency in languages like Python, Java, or Ruby with a willingness to use Go).
* Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Proven experience with configuration and change management using Ansible, Terraform, or equivalent tools.
* Data Systems: Experience in basic database troubleshooting and management of MySQL, Cassandra, NoSQL, and Columnar databases (analyzing logs, managing processes).
* Soft Skills:



 -Self-motivated and detail-oriented with a process-driven mindset.
 



 -Strong communication skills to collaborate effectively across diverse technical domains.
 



 -A strong service-oriented mindset: you find genuine satisfaction in unblocking colleagues and helping other teams succeed.
 



 - Empathy and active listening: the ability to translate a developer's frustration into a constructive platform improvement.
 



  





**Growth Opportunities** 




 This role provides an excellent platform for professional development within a cutting-edge technical environment. You will have the unique chance to:
 


* Work on high-impact projects that directly affect our core application estate.
* Learn from and collaborate with senior infrastructure architects.
* Master the tools and methodologies that define modern site reliability and systems engineering.
* Enjoy a fun, helpful, and challenging team culture that actively supports your technical deep-dives and continuous learning.



  





**The location: Stockholm Sweden** 




  





**Right to work requirements:** 




 This role is limited to persons with indefinite right to work in Sweden.
 



  





**Our Benefits: Global vision, local impact** 




 At OSTTRA, our benefits philosophy offers a market-competitive package that reflects our position as an industry leader. We want to ensure you feel valued, secure, and empowered wherever you are in the world.
 



 Our approach is built on three core pillars:
 


* **Global foundations** 
 : We provide a baseline of excellence across all our offices, focusing on comprehensive Health & Wellness, Financial Security, and Work-Life Balance. No matter your location, you are part of a culture that prioritizes your holistic well-being.
* **Locally tailored** 
 : We benchmark in each market to ensure our packages are genuinely competitive where you live and work.
* **Flexibility and growth:** 
 We empower you to work in a way that suits your life. This includes a hybrid working model, generous leave policies, and a commitment to your continuous professional development.



  





**The Trust employee ownership plan** 




 Every employee at OSTTRA is an owner, and a member of The Trust, our employee ownership programme. Our distinct culture encourages colleagues to think and act like owners and raise the bar constantly.
 



 This plan reflects our belief that when we work together to build a stronger, more valuable organisation, then all colleagues should benefit from the value we create.
 



  





**About OSTTRA** 




 We build and operate the infrastructure that the world’s post-trade financial system runs on. Launched in 2021, we have unified more than 20 years of heritage and expertise from four industry leaders into a single, integrated business.
 



 Today,we operate a trusted network that connects thousands of market participants to streamline end-to-end workflows, from trade capture through portfolio optimisation.We translate industry change into workable process, engineering an open, intelligent ecosystem that removes friction and reduces risk for the global markets.
 



  





 Our distinct ownership culture is underpinned by four behaviours: boldness, curiosity, accountability, and collaboration. In 2025 we were acquired by KKR, one of the world’s most successful private equity firms, and are on a journey of growth and impact.
 



 Joining our team now is a unique opportunity - you will help to shape the next generation of post-trade, transform shared infrastructure into shared benefit, and accelerate your own career. Learn more at
 
 www.osttra.com
 
 .
 



  





**Equal opportunity employer statement at OSTTRA:**




 We are committed to fostering a connected and engaged workplace where all colleagues have access to opportunities based on their skills, experience, and contributions. Our hiring practices emphasise fairness, transparency, and merit, ensuring that we attract and retain the best talent. By valuing different perspectives and promoting a culture of respect and collaboration, we drive and operate the infrastructure that the world’s post-trade financial system runs on

title: DevOps Engineer/Systemutvecklare
description:
*Vill du göra samhällsnytta och bidra till Sveriges oberoende i världen? Just nu söker vi en erfaren systemutvecklare för utveckling av interna system och funktioner. På FRA är varje arbetsuppgift en del av något större. Är du den vi söker kan vi erbjuda ett varierande arbete i en kunskapsintensiv miljö där du får vara med och göra skillnad varje dag.*
 Ditt uppdrag
   

  

 Som systemutvecklare hör du till avdelningen för Teknik. Avdelningen har till uppgift att förse FRA med de tekniska system som behövs för verksamheten. Avdelningen utvecklar, anskaffar och driftsätter nya system samt sköter drift och underhåll av befintliga system.
   

  

 Du blir en del av ett utvecklingsteam som ansvarar för myndighetens utvecklingsmiljö och de digitala verktygen som stödjer utvecklingsarbetet och driftsäkerheten. Tillsammans skapar ni förutsättningar för hög kvalitet, automatisering och effektiva arbetssätt som hjälper verksamheten att leverera värde på ett säkert och hållbart sätt.
   

  

 För att utveckla och stärka dessa förutsättningar arbetar du med att införa, konfigurera och förvalta lösningar som förenklar utvecklarnas vardag. Du anpassar verktyg efter användarnas behov, bidrar till effektiva utvecklingsprocesser och säkerställer att lösningarna möter myndighetens krav på IT-säkerhet.
   

  

 Rollen innebär ett nära samarbete med både interna och externa kontaktytor, där du fungerar som en viktig länk mellan teknik, användarbehov och verksamhet. Genom att fånga upp behov, driva förbättringsinitiativ och utveckla arbetssätt bidrar du till att verktyg och processer fortsätter att skapa värde, både idag och i framtiden.
   

  

 För att trivas i rollen har du en god problemlösande analysförmåga, initiativtagande och har ett genuint intresse för att utveckla både teknik och arbetssätt. Du uppskattar samarbete, känner dig hemma i en agil miljö och motiveras av att hitta lösningar som gör skillnad för både utvecklare och verksamhet.
   

  

 Resor kan förekomma i tjänsten.
   

  

 Du kan
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Som Har**
* Eftergymnasial utbildning med IT-inriktning eller motsvarande kompetens förvärvad genom arbetslivserfarenhet i minst 3 år
* Arbetat med systemutveckling under de senaste tre åren
* Erfarenhet av Linuxbaserade utvecklingsmiljöerErfarenhet av automatisering och provisionering med verktyg som Terraform, Ansible eller motsvarande
* Erfarenhet av containerbaserade plattformar, exempelvis Kubernetes
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig på svenska och engelska i tal och skrift


 Vi ser det som meriterande om du har kunskap och erfarenhet av ett eller flera områden:
   

  

* Agila arbetssätt såsom SAFe eller Scrum
* Objektorienterad programmering
* Nätverk, logisk nätverkskonfiguration och brandväggar
* Molnplattformar såsom AWS, Azure eller GCP
* Utveckling och förvaltning av CI/CD-pipelines


 Du är
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Med Följande Egenskaper**
* Samarbetsförmåga – Du arbetar bra med andra människor. Relaterar sig till dem på ett lyhört och smidigt sätt. Lyssnar, kommunicerar och löser konflikter på ett konstruktivt sätt
* Initiativtagande – Du tar initiativ, sätter igång aktiviteter och uppnår resultat
* Mål- och resultatorienterad – Du arbetar mot mål och fokuserar på resultat. Styrs av mål-och resultattänkande i prioriteringar, planering och agerande. Förändrar sin inriktning när målen ändrar sig


 Om FRA
   

  

 FRA har omvärlden som sin arena där varje arbetsuppgift är en del av något större. På ett eller annat sätt får du som medarbetare vara med och bidra till att skydda Sveriges integritet och oberoende i världen. Du är med och gör skillnad på riktigt, varje dag.
   

  

 Vår uppgift som underrättelsetjänst är att skydda Sverige mot de allvarligaste hot som kan riktas mot ett samhälle – militära angrepp, terrorism och cyberattacker. Vi skyddar det viktigaste vi har – vår trygghet, vår demokrati och vårt sätt att leva. Vi är experter på cyberområdet och signalspaning samt en larmklocka för militära hot.
   

  

 FRA leder sedan 2024 Nationellt cybersäkerhetscenter, NCSC vars uppdrag är att utveckla och stärka Sveriges förmåga att förebygga, upptäcka och hantera cyberhot.
   

  

 Som nyanställd på FRA får du en omfattande introduktionsutbildning och kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling, vilket ger dig möjlighet att utveckla dina färdigheter och bli ännu bättre på det du brinner för. Vår organisation är mångfacetterad och består av olika kompetenser och personligheter, där varje individ har unika egenskaper och förutsättningar. Vi strävar efter att skapa en miljö där alla känner sig välkomna och trygga. Utöver en stimulerande arbetsmiljö erbjuder vi också ett attraktivt förmånspaket, som du kan läsa mer om på www.fra.se . För mer information om Nationellt cybersäkerhetscenter besök www.ncsc.se
   

  

 För din trygghet. För vår demokrati. Varje dag. Året om.
   

  

 Mer information
   

  

 Arbetsplatsen är belägen i närheten av Drottningholm, ca 10 min från Brommaplan. Svenskt medborgarskap är ett krav då anställning vid FRA innebär placering i säkerhetsklass. Innan en anställning kommer du att säkerhetsprövas i enlighet med säkerhetsskyddslagens bestämmelser. Med anställning följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. Vi tillämpar sex månaders provanställning. I denna rekryteringsprocess kommer arbetsprov/tester att tillämpas. För mer information är du välkommen att kontakta rekrytering@fra.se. Fackliga företrädare nås på 010-557 46 00.
   

  

 Vi tar emot ansökningar via www.fra.se
 **senast 2026-07-19.**
 Referensnummer
 **2025FRA1629-12.**
 Då vi inför denna rekrytering redan tagit ställning till de rekryteringskanaler vi önskar använda undanber vi oss att bli kontaktade för ytterligare erbjudanden om annonserings- och rekryteringshjälp.
   

  

 Sökord:
 *DevOps, systemutvecklare, Kubernetes, OpenShift, Sys Admin, mjukvaruutvecklare, Platform Engineer, systemadministratör, IT-säkerhet, Developer Experience, Linux*

title: Cloud Software Developer
description:
**Overview**
 We are seeking a talented and passionate Senior Software Developer for our back-end to join our global Azure development team. In this role, you will be responsible for Azure infrastructure such as code, pipelines and cloud architecture . You will also be developing and deploying scalable and reliable cloud-based solutions using node.js and Azure services. You will work close to other developers, testers and Product Owners in India and Sweden, to build and maintain our cloud infrastructure and applications. A key focus will be on ensuring the solutions we build are production-ready, adhering to best practice for security, performance and maintainability. You will contribute to the evolution of our cloud architecture and play a vital role in driving our cloud strategy.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Design, develop and deploy cloud-native applications using Node.js and Azure services
* Write clean, testable and well-documented code
* Ensure code adheres to established coding standards and best practices for production environments
* Implement CI/CD pipelines using GitHub actions.
* Proactively identify and address potential performance bottlenecks and security vulnerabilities
* Provide technical support and guidance to other team members
* Contribute to development of internal tools
* Design and maintain our Cloud infrastructure including infrastructure as code


**Requirements**
* 10+ years of relevant work experience within Back-end development
* 3+ years of experience in Azure DevOps, bicep or terraform infrastructure as code
* 3+ years of experience in Node.js, typescript, JavaScript
* 3+ years of experience in CI/CD pipelines
* 3+ years of experience in Azure cloud services
* 5+ years in containerization
* Experience in monitoring and logging tools


**Soft Skills**
* Strong team player with a collaborative mindset — able to work effectively with cross-functional and globally distributed teams.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, essential for clarity and alignment in a global development environment.
* High initiative and strong problem-solving ability — able to take ownership and drive work forward independently and together with the team.
* Comfortable working in a dynamic and evolving environment, with the ability to adapt quickly and handle shifting priorities.
* Flexible, solution-oriented, and open to feedback, with a continuous improvement mindset.
* Able to handle disagreements and discussions constructively and respectfully, supporting strong team relationships.
* Enjoys sharing knowledge and mentoring other team members, contributing to collective learning and team growth.

title: Incident & Operations Engineer III
description:
Sinch is pioneering the way the world communicates. More than 150,000 businesses — including Google, Uber, Paypal, Visa, Tinder, and many others — rely on Sinch’s Customer Communications Cloud to power engaging customer experiences through mobile messaging, voice, and email.


Whether you need to verify users or craft omnichannel campaigns, Sinch makes it easy. Our AI-infused Super Network, APIs, and applications ensure you can connect with your customers reliably and securely, at every step of their journey.


At Sinch we “Dream Big”, “Win Together”, “Keep it simple”, and “Make it Happen”. These values are our foundation!


**DESCRIPTION**

We are looking for an Incident & Operations Engineer III to join our Technical Operations Center team in Kalmar. In this role, you will focus on incident management, ensuring smooth operations with internal systems, and providing critical support in resolving technical issues. Additionally, you will play a key role in supporting our operator and provider partners to maintain high-quality service delivery.


We tackle every challenge together as a team, sharing the workload no matter how complex it gets! With our 24/7 operations, we are dedicated to resolving incidents swiftly and keeping business disruptions to an absolute minimum. To help us maintain this high standard of service, your day-to-day responsibilities will include:


* Collaborate with internal teams to ensure smooth resolution of issues and improved ways of working.
* Conduct in-depth troubleshooting at both the software and network layers to identify and resolve technical problems.
* Act as the technical lead and incident commander for our core services during incidents.
* Monitor the health of our global infrastructure and ensure incidents are managed and escalated as needed.
* Document and track alerts and incidents for future improvements.
* Leads blameless Post-Implementations Reviews. Partners directly with platform/software engineering teams to implement structural fixes and optimization.
* Transitions manual procedural tasks into automated workflows.

**REQUIREMENTS**

Sinch is a tech company through and through where great ideas are listened to and often implemented - we want you to feel part of the bigger picture!


This role is full of challenges and offers plenty of opportunities to grow. To thrive here, you’ll need a curious mindset and a passion for learning. A “can-do” attitude will help you turn obstacles into opportunities and keep you moving forward.


* **Education** : A Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related technical field (or equivalent professional experience).
* **Communication** : Excellent written and verbal English communication skills, with the ability to explain technical issues clearly to both internal teams and external partners.
* **Problem-Solving** : Strong analytical skills and a methodical approach to troubleshooting software or system alerts.
* **Composure** : The ability to stay calm and focused in a high-pressure environment, especially when managing multiple tasks or urgent incidents.
* **Flexibility** : Ability to work in a 24/7 shift rotation, including nights and weekends.
* **Location** : Able to work onsite at our office in Kalmar, Sweden.

In addition to the above, it is a strong advantage if you have:


* Hands-on experience with incident management and platform monitoring.
* Understanding of network fundamentals such as TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/S, and BGP.
* Experience collaborating effectively with internal teams and stakeholders, demonstrating accountability and the ability to take ownership of issues.
* Knowledge with UNIX and Linux systems.
* Practical knowledge of cloud infrastructure or containerization.
* Background in telecoms and wireless communication.
* Understanding of mobile communications standards like GSM and 4G.
* Ability to understand and write code/scripts.
* Knowledge within different type of databases.
* Strong mentoring abilities.

Our corporate language is English, please submit your application in English


**OUR HIRING PROCESS**

We are committed to ensuring a recruitment process that is fair, objective, consistent, and inclusive. Our approach includes structured, competency-based interviews designed to evaluate your skills, experience, and qualifications relevant to the role. At times, we may include a data-driven assessment to enhance our hiring success and identify candidates likely to excel.


We believe in a two-way process and encourage you to ask questions throughout the journey. If this role isn't what you're looking for, please explore the other opportunities listed on our career page: https://www.sinch.com/careers/.


No matter who you are, we hope you find an exciting path forward - hopefully with us!


**Salary**

The monthly salary for this position is between 40 000 SEK to 50 000 SEK. Factors which may affect pay within this range may include skills, education, experience, and other qualifications.
